core_takeaway: Agent_1 beat the last position because it claimed the highest-value anchor, Jewel, from the opening and made that claim compatible with others' side payments. Agent_5 also valued Jewel, but publicly accepted the role of Pencil recipient once Plan A made Pencil its consolation prize.
why_or_counterexample: The first position looks advantaged mainly through agenda control: Agent_1 spoke first, framed Jewel as the central object, and got others to negotiate over the remaining items rather than contesting Jewel directly. The counterexample is Round 1, where self-serving concentrated proposals failed; the advantage only converted once Agent_1 and most others converged on the distributed Plan A.

phase_notes.public_discussion: Present. The public talk quickly normalized a Jewel-first frame, then narrowed to Plan A with Jewel for Agent_1 and each other agent receiving a targeted item.
phase_notes.private_thinking: Present. Ten private-thinking entries diagnose the same hinge: a credible Agent_1 Jewel commitment plus a transparent post-Jewel allocation.
phase_notes.formal_proposals: Present. Round 1 proposals split between Plan A and all-remaining-items-to-Agent_2; Round 2 moved four of five proposals to Plan A.
phase_notes.private_voting: Present. Votes show the concentrated Agent_2 allocation was unacceptable to agents left with zero, while Plan A became unanimously acceptable in Round 2.
phase_notes.reflection: Present. Five reflections identify public commitment, Apple/Pencil timing, and a verifiable Plan A as the path to consensus.

mechanisms.theme: First speaker captured the focal prize
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_1 used the opening move to define Jewel as the prize and to invite bargaining around everyone else's compensation. Because Jewel was also the highest-valued item for Agent_1, preserving that anchor produced the large first-over-last utility gap.

evidence.quote: Jewel is the prize I value most (49)
Jewel-first coalition:
Jewel must be my next destination

mechanisms.theme: Side payments turned Jewel concession into a coalition
mechanisms.explanation: Other agents did not simply concede Jewel for nothing. They accepted Jewel for Agent_1 when the remaining items could satisfy their own concrete asks: Stone for Agent_2, Apple for Agent_3, and Pencil for Agent_5.

evidence.quote: publicly commit to getting Stone to me promptly
publicly back Plan A as the baseline if Jewel stays with Agent_1
Plan A as the baseline if you publicly commit to Jewel staying with you
Plan A achieves Pencil in Round 1 and secures Jewel with Agent_1

mechanisms.theme: Round 1 shows the advantage was conditional
mechanisms.explanation: The first-position advantage was not automatic. Agent_1's first formal proposal concentrated all non-Jewel items with Agent_2, and that structure failed badly because most agents received nothing. Plan A also had one holdout at first.

evidence.quote: Proposal #1: 1 accept, 4 reject
Only allocates Stone to Agent_2 (44)
No proposal achieved unanimous acceptance

mechanisms.theme: Credibility and public commitment unlocked Plan A
mechanisms.explanation: Private and reflection material repeatedly identifies the same mechanism: agents would accept Agent_1 keeping Jewel only if the commitment was public and tied to an auditable Plan A allocation. This converted the earlier framing into actual consensus.

evidence.quote: Public commitment from Agent_1 to keep Jewel
Plan A cannot be anchored publicly
public commitment from Agent_1 to keep Jewel is the key hinge for credibility
Plan A is the strongest path to unanimous acceptance

mechanisms.theme: Last position accepted a lower-value consolation
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_5, the last position, also wanted Jewel but settled for Pencil under Plan A. That kept Agent_5 in the coalition while leaving Agent_1 with Jewel, producing the final 44.1 versus 19.8 outcome.

evidence.quote: Jewel and Pencil
Allocates Pencil to me (Agent_5)
CONSENSUS REACHED
